title: Cyclobalanopsis dinghuensis
intro: Cyclobalanopsis dinghuensis, also known as Dinghu oak, is a species of tree belonging to the genus Cyclobalanopsis in the family Fagaceae. The health benefits of Cyclobalanopsis dinghuensis include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various diseases such as arthritis, cardiovascular disorders, and infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the most common herbal preparations made with Cyclobalanopsis dinghuensis include decoctions, infusions, and granules, which are used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Cyclobalanopsis dinghuensis is a species of tree in the beech family, Fagaceae. It is a deciduous tree native to China, specifically found in the Dinghu Mountains in Guangdong Province. The classification of Cyclobalanopsis dinghuensis places it within the genus Cyclobalanopsis and the family Fagaceae. This species is closely related to other members of the Fagaceae family, such as the beech and oak trees. Cyclobalanopsis dinghuensis is also sometimes classified as a subspecies of Cyclobalanopsis glabra.
